page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9ae.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The x86_64 architecture operates with a single thread per core configuration, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No graphics processing units (GPUs) are integrated into this hardware profile.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high memory density make this instance suitable for workloads requiring predictable CPU execution and large memory footprints. The lack of hyperthreading and local storage represents a design tradeoff, prioritizing memory capacity and dedicated processing over local disk I/O speed. Appropriate workloads include in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and memory-intensive data processing applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9ae.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at 2.25 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating with a single thread per core under the KVM hypervisor, it provides 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on external storage solutions and utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. This hardware configuration is designed for workloads requiring high memory capacity and dedicated processing resources without hyperthreading overhead. It is suited for in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
